docs.title: Inova Monitoring — Documentation
docs.text: Inova Monitoring is a lightweight Python platform for monitoring Inova Apps instances in real-time.

docs.title: ✨ Features
docs.text:
• PostgreSQL Query Interface — Run any SQL query and see results appear live in a dynamic table.
  • Real-time WebSocket Communication — Persistent connection with typed hexagonal message envelopes.
  • Prometheus Metrics Integration — Visualize metrics from any Prometheus endpoint.
  • SSO Authentication — OAuth2/OIDC-ready access control.
  • Environment-based Configuration — All settings managed via .env.

docs.title: 🚀 Quick Start
docs.text:
# Clone & install
git clone git@github.com:JZacharie/inova_monitoring.git
cd inova_monitoring
uv sync --all-extras --dev

# Configure
cp .env.example .env

# Run
uv run uvicorn src.inova_monitoring.main:app --reload

Open http://localhost:8000 to access the dashboard.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: Inova Monitoring follows the Hexagonal Architecture pattern (Ports & Adapters) to keep the domain logic independent of infrastructure concerns.

docs.title: Layers
docs.text:
Browser (WebSocket Client)
        │   WebSocket /ws
        ▼
FastAPI (main.py)          ← Interface Layer
        │
        ▼
messages.py                ← Port: typed envelopes (Pydantic)
   QueryRequest / QueryResult / QueryError / WelcomeMessage
        │
        ▼
database.py                ← Adapter: SQLAlchemy → PostgreSQL
        │
        ▼
PostgreSQL                 ← Infrastructure

docs.title: File Structure
docs.text: File Role main.py FastAPI app, WebSocket /ws endpoint messages.py Pydantic hexagonal message envelopes database.py SQLAlchemy engine, raw query execution config.py Pydantic-Settings, loads from .env templates/index.html Dashboard HTML + WS client JS static/style.css Glassmorphism dark-mode CSS

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: All configuration is managed via environment variables. Copy .env.example to .env and set the values.

docs.title: PostgreSQL
docs.text: Variable Default Description POSTGRES_HOST localhost Server host POSTGRES_PORT 5432 Server port POSTGRES_USER postgres Username POSTGRES_PASSWORD password Password POSTGRES_DB inova_monitoring Database name

docs.title: Prometheus
docs.text: Variable Default Description PROMETHEUS_URL http://localhost:9090 Base URL for metrics

docs.title: SSO (OAuth2 / OIDC)
docs.text: Variable Description SSO_CLIENT_ID Application client ID SSO_CLIENT_SECRET Application client secret SSO_AUTH_URL Authorization endpoint URL SSO_TOKEN_URL Token endpoint URL

docs.title: Application
docs.text: Variable Default Description DEBUG true Enable debug mode API_SECRET_KEY (change me) Session signing key

docs.title: WebSocket Protocol
docs.text: The backend exposes a single WebSocket endpoint at /ws. Communication uses typed, self-describing JSON message envelopes.

docs.title: query_request
docs.text:
Execute an SQL query.

{
  "type": "query_request",
  "payload": {
    "sql": "SELECT * FROM users LIMIT 10"
  }
}

docs.title: welcome
docs.text:
Sent immediately on connection.

{
  "type": "welcome",
  "payload": {
    "message": "Connected to Inova Monitoring",
    "version": "0.1.0"
  }
}

docs.title: query_error
docs.text:
Sent when a query fails (parse error or database error).

{
  "type": "query_error",
  "payload": {
    "detail": "relation \"missing_table\" does not exist"
  }
}

docs.title: Connection Lifecycle
docs.text:
1. Client connects → Server sends welcome.
  2. Client sends query_request → Server responds with query_result or query_error.
  3. If the connection drops, the client automatically reconnects after 3 seconds.
